transcript.text: Good afternoon, Moe.

I'm doing pretty well.

Busy.
Busy, busy.
But that's nothing new.
Yeah, this AI world we're in moves pretty fast, doesn't it?
Yeah, man, it does.
It's exciting, but it's also like constantly changing, obviously ever-evolving.
And I'm just trying to stay on top of that.
Jordan, who's also joining us, he's really been spearheading a lot of the AI efforts here within our marketing org.

I'm on the AI governance board here at Vanco, and so I'd say like our team and, of course, tech and product are like the two that's been kind of constantly going through and saying, hey, we'd like to use this tool.
Hey, we'd like to use this tool.
And then being on the governance board side is like also balancing like how do we make sure that we're governing this thing the right way while allowing expansion and adoption.
A hundred percent.
Yeah, there's always more to learn.
That's for sure.

I'm guessing that Jen gave you both some context on our conversation about GrowthX, and that's why we're here today.
Yeah, yeah.
She's like, hey, you got to talk to GrowthX.
Let's see what they, or let's definitely chat with them so we can potentially look at scaling what we're doing.
Perfect.
Yeah, bring in Jen in now.
She just jumped in the waiting room.
But as a quick intro on my side, I'm the founding AE here at GrowthX, so I work hand-in-hand with our CEO, Marcel.

And the high level on us is that we're an end-to-end partner that does everything in terms of like SEO and GEO strategy, coming up with a roadmap of assignments, and then creating content and staging and publishing for you on a daily basis.
So a lot of times, you know, the clients that we work with, they see us as like extensions of their content team.
And I would say the biggest...
This problem that we're solving is just this concept of, you know, how can you leverage AI workflows, but also experts in the loop so that you can scale quality, right?
Like no hallucinations, no AI slop, really just being able to create content similar at a higher caliber to what you're doing today, but at a much larger scale.
So maybe we could just go around the room and do some quick intros and maybe like what you, like, what would be most helpful to make sure that you get out of today's call?
I can start.
I'm Jen.
I don't know if you remember me, Tyler.
met when I was at Raptor Technologies and I told you like, hey, thanks for taking the call, but this is probably for a future company.
So anyway, it was an informative, do you remember?
Okay.
Absolutely remember.
Yeah.

And so then I'm like, get here at Vanco and I'll let them introduce themselves before I say what I was going to say about how awesome Jordan's been doing.
But, you y go ahead and introduce yourselves.
No worries.
So Moe McLeod, director of digital marketing, essentially heading up growth.
Big picture for me is making sure to support Jordan.
I'm intrigued by it being end-to-end.
I come from agency world, so a lot of that is really unique.
then obviously seeing AI scalability using tech, I kind of, part of my purview is marketing tech, ops to a certain degree.
And so, yeah, being able to see how this can support us in Jordan's efforts, I'm super intrigued.
Awesome.
Great to meet you, Moe.
Yeah.
Jordan, I've been here for, I think this is going to be my seven-year anniversary.
Anniversary next week.
So we've been doing Vanco stuff for a while.
I do a lot of the SEO just because we needed to scale.
We've been scaling quite a bit with AI, but we don't have the full complex workflows like the type that you'd see in N8N, partially because approval is an issue here.
Got it.
What have you used to create, like, or what have you used from an AI perspective to help with content creation?
I mean, I usually have, like, a sequence of prompts.
So, I mean, I have to kind of run the sequence that I would do.
I would have AI do, like, ideally, I'd put it all in N8N and have it run the sequence.
But I have sequences of prompts for it or GPTs that we use or...
Okay, understood.
And you execute each of those prompts in ChatGPT and other LLMs or tools or just ChatGPT?
In other LLMs.
I did get Poe in through before they kind of locked things up a little bit here, just as a way to be able to use the different LLMs as needed.
Yeah.
Okay, got it.
And I imagine like you're using some of those prompts for the brief that gets generated or the assignment, right?
The deep research.
What about drafting the content?
Do you use AI in the drafting?
The drafts, the content.

And I just have like an editor, at least for the new content, an editor to make, just kind of review it because I think I've been able to get it without a lot of hallucinations, but there's always like one or two of them in there per piece, like that it is.
It glosses over.

And I guess, Jordan, since you have been able to successfully leverage AI with the content creation process, and I'm sure that's had a benefit on scaling as well, where would you say you're still experiencing challenges or would benefit from the help of a partner like GrowthX just to help me sort of center in on that?
I mean, for me, the updating existing content actually is more time consuming, I would say, just because the balance of doing that correctly with AI is a little bit more of a challenge.
Also, we can't like automate it into HubSpot, like I can't like have it, create it, put it into HubSpot.
I have to actually manually put it into HubSpot because, I mean, there's connectors with GBT and Claude, but I don't have those approvals.
Okay.
Understood.
Understood.
Super helpful.
Well, I'll go through a quick presentation on growthx.
I'll share some like content examples of what we've been able to create for some of our customers.
And then I'll actually go into our platform and show you how the workflows are leveraged.
Feel free to interrupt me as I go.
Happy to keep this conversational and answer any questions that you might have.
But the high level on growthx, just as the search landscape has changed from software for XYZ to here's my life story, tell me which platform or software is best for me.
One thing that we've seen on our side is you just need a much larger surface area of content to be the best answer to all of those questions that your ICP might have.
Right.
And also, if you're not in the answer, they're almost treating it like you don't exist.
And so the, you know, the ROI is definitely there if you can show up in AI.
Answers, AI search visits are 4.4 times more likely to convert.
They spend three times as long.
But then the question becomes, like, how do you scale quality?
How do you create content that's relevant?
And how do you create content that's optimized for LLMs?
And so our approach, what we do is we take the most important factors for GEO, and we make sure that our workflows are addressing each of these factors in detail.
So when you think about research, we are leveraging a lot of different deep research agents.
We've built our own agentic research agent.
And so then you're not stuck with, right, perplexity deep research or ChatGVT.
You're combining a lot of different models to make sure that the research is very thorough.
When we think about how we can customize this, we can make sure that we do research against specific sources.
We can create these artifacts that give our workflows a ton of context up front.
So that the content's more personalized to your brand voice and any guidelines that you have.
And then we do a lot of content refreshes.
We recommend refreshing content at least every six months, ideally every three.
And to your point, Jordan, you know, we have workflows that are specifically around refreshing content so that we can do this at scale as well.
Part of the reason that folks are going with GrowthX and that we're experiencing a ton of demand is our CEO and founder has always believed that brands should act more like publishers.
He's never had a big paid ads budget.
He's always wanted to build an organic content engine.
And so whether he was, you know, the marketing leader at HashiCorp from six to 100 million or the CMO at scale through the acquisition by Meta, he's always gone through this process.
And then at Deepgram, which is where he was the CMO before starting GrowthX, he replicated that process by building AI workflows for each step of the process.
And so that's the GrowthX origins.
Story is the workshops that, you know, Jen found out about, you know, that led to like a bunch of demand around, hey, this looks really hard.
Like, can you help me figure out, right, how to leverage AI to do these things?

sure.

Yeah.

How would that work?

How would that work?

No problem.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Evaluate GrowthX's AI content scaling service for Vanco's marketing needs.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=67.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Vanco's Need:** Vanco requires a scalable content solution for two sites post-January's business unit split. A key challenge is manually updating existing content, which is more time-consuming than creating new content.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=1080.0)
  - [**GrowthX's Offer:** GrowthX proposed an end-to-end service: strategy, creation (5 articles/week), and publishing. The model is a hybrid of AI workflows and expert oversight to ensure quality.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=90.0)
  - [**Strategic Mismatch:** GrowthX's model builds workflows on its proprietary platform, while Vanco's AI Governance Board requires a "closed loop" solution built directly within Vanco's secure, internal infrastructure.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=1646.0)
  - [**Path Forward:** GrowthX will provide a detailed workflow demo to clarify how Vanco could leverage the partnership's outputs (artifacts, assignments) for internal use, addressing the strategic mismatch.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=1646.0)

## Topics

### Vanco's Content Scaling Challenge

  - [**Current Process:** Jordan manually uses prompt sequences and GPTs for new content.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=270.0)
  - [**Primary Bottleneck:** Manually updating existing content is more time-consuming than creating new content.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=363.0)
  - [**Automation Blocker:** Vanco's AI Governance Board prevents direct HubSpot integrations with LLMs (e.g., ChatGPT, Claude), forcing manual publishing.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=363.0)
  - [**Strategic Context:** A business unit split in January creates two sites:](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=1080.0)
      - [**Existing Site:** Faith & Nonprofit content.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=1080.0)
      - [**New Site:** Education content (new domain, requires rapid authority building).](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=1080.0)
  - [**Content Goal:** Increase comparative content for high-converting, lower-volume keywords, especially for the EDU market.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=1020.0)

### GrowthX's Proposed Solution

  - [**Service Model:** An end-to-end partner for SEO/GEO strategy, content creation, and publishing.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=90.0)
  - [**Methodology:** A hybrid model using proprietary AI workflows with expert oversight.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=903.0)
      - [**AI:** Uses agentic research, custom knowledge graphs, and multiple LLMs (e.g., Perplexity, Claude, specialized tools like Tavly).](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=841.0)
      - [**Experts:** A dedicated team (Managing Director, Editor, Content Manager) ensures quality and brand alignment.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=903.0)
  - [**Process:**](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=960.0)
      - [**Phase 1 (8-week sprint):** SEO/GEO audit, strategy, roadmap, and \~10 articles to calibrate quality.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=960.0)
      - [**Phase 2 (Ongoing):** 5 articles per week, with work split across Vanco's two sites.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=1398.0)
  - [**Pricing:**](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=960.0)
      - [**Phase 1:** $30k (startups) or $40k (enterprises).](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=960.0)
      - [**Phase 2:** $15k/month for 5 articles/week.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=995.0)
  - [**Analytics:**](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=665.0)
      - [**Current:** Uses a Scrunch dashboard.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=665.0)
      - [**Future (Jan 2026):** Launching a proprietary platform, with access retained by clients post-contract.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=665.0)

### Strategic Mismatch: Infrastructure Ownership

  - [**Vanco's Requirement:** A "closed loop" solution built within Vanco's secure, internal infrastructure.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=1716.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Security concerns (payments industry) and a desire to build long-term, transferable internal capabilities.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=1716.0)
  - [**GrowthX's Model:** Workflows are built and run on GrowthX's proprietary platform.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=1792.0)
      - [**Client Deliverables:** Vanco would own the content, artifacts (e.g., persona docs), and assignment roadmap.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=1810.0)
      - [**Workflow Access:** Workflows are not exportable but can be transparently explained.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=1646.0)
  - [**Outcome:** Jen Hoitsma expressed that a partnership would be more compelling if it involved building the infrastructure *in Vanco's environment*, not just delivering content.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=1716.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Tyler Pavlas (GrowthX):**](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=1907.0)
      - [Confirm data access policy for clients post-contract.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=730.0)
      - [Send Vanco a list of tools used in GrowthX's workflows.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=841.0)
      - [Provide a detailed demo of GrowthX's workflows to clarify how Vanco could leverage the partnership's outputs (artifacts, assignments) for internal use.](https://fathom.video/share/pQ1yi3bcGgCam5vRGy1ZB5dsb-PB915V?tab=summary&timestamp=1646.0)

action_items.description: Confirm post-engagement LLM monitoring data access; update Jen
Email Moe, Jen, Jordan: tool list, content examples, workflow demo video
Confirm 5 blogs/wk split across 2 sites; update Moe
